title: The Snob Within: Why We Obsess Over Status
subtitle: 「你做什么工作？」这个问题为何让人焦虑

paragraphs.en: Why are we so worried about our careers? Partly it's to do with money, but there's a psychological aspect to our fears as well. We worry because we suspect — not wrongly — that the world is full of a frightening sort of person ready to judge us ruthlessly and swiftly: a person we can call a snob. A snob is anyone who takes a relatively small part of us and uses it to come to a rigid conclusion about how much of their attention we deserve.
paragraphs.zh: 我们为何如此担忧职业生涯？部分与金钱有关，但我们的恐惧也有心理层面的原因。我们担忧是因为我们怀疑——这怀疑没错——世界上充满了一种可怕的人，他们准备无情而迅速地评判我们：我们可以称之为势利小人。势利小人是指抓住我们身上相对微小的一部分，便据此武断地决定我们应得多少关注的人。

paragraphs.en: In the past, that might be your ancestry and royal connections. Nowadays, the snob cares about one thing only: what you do for a living. This explains why the first question we will be asked in any new social context is 'What do you do?' and according to how we answer, snobs will either welcome us with broad smiles, or leave us in the cold.
paragraphs.zh: 过去，那可能是你的血统和皇室关系。如今，势利小人只关心一件事：你以何为生。这解释了为何在任何新的社交场合我们被问的第一个问题都是“你是做什么的？”根据我们的回答，势利小人要么笑脸相迎，要么冷眼相待。

paragraphs.en: And that is why we are fired up by such a desperate urge to achieve and impress. Sometimes our behaviour is mistaken for greed and vanity, but it is more than this. A lot of our interest in fancy cars, jobs and houses has nothing to do with materialism. It has to do with a hunger for the respect and esteem that is only available in our societies through the acquisition of material goods.
paragraphs.zh: 正因如此，我们被一种拼命取得成就、给人留下深刻印象的渴望所驱使。有时我们的行为被误认为贪婪和虚荣，但远不止于此。我们对豪车、工作和房子的许多兴趣与物质主义无关，而是与对尊重和敬意的渴望有关，而这种渴望在我们的社会中只能通过获取物质财富来获得。

paragraphs.en: It isn't the goods themselves we seek, it is the love we stand to gain through our possession of them. The next time we see someone driving a Ferrari, we shouldn't condemn them for their greed, we should pity them for the intensity of their need for love from the world. At the root of snobbery is a lack of imagination and confidence about how to decide who in the world is valuable.
paragraphs.zh: 我们追求的不是物品本身，而是通过拥有它们可能获得的爱。下次看到有人开法拉利，我们不应谴责其贪婪，而应怜悯他们如此强烈地需要世人的爱。势利眼的根源在于，对于如何判断世上谁有价值，他们缺乏想象力和信心。

paragraphs.en: The snobs are brutally misguided in their beliefs about how superior individuals can be identified. For snobs, only the already acclaimed and successful are worthy of respect. There is no room in their timid minds to imagine that someone might be clever, kind or good — yet somehow have been overlooked by society, their qualities lying hidden beneath an unfamiliar veil.
paragraphs.zh: 势利小人在如何识别优秀个体的信念上严重误入歧途。对他们而言，只有已受赞誉和成功的人才值得尊重。他们狭隘胆怯的头脑中，容不下这样的想象：某人可能聪明、善良、优秀，却不知何故被社会完全忽视，其品质隐藏在陌生的面纱之下。

paragraphs.en: The true answer to snobbery is not to say that there is no such thing as a better or worse person, but to insist that better or worse exist in constantly unexpected places and carry none of the outward signs of distinction. And because we are such poor judges of the worth of others, our ultimate duty remains to be kind, good, curious and imaginative about pretty much everyone who ever crosses our path.
paragraphs.zh: 对势利眼的真正回应，不是说没有更好或更差的人，而是坚持认为好坏存在于不断出人意料之处，且不带有任何外在的区别标志。正因为我们如此不善于评判他人的价值，我们最终的责任仍是对几乎每一个与我们有交集的人保持友善、善良、好奇和富有想象力。
